original_title: Update hpc-watchdog.sh for current model deployment. infrastructure/hpc-watchdog.sh checks hardcoded, outdated endpoints: wrong model names, wrong ports (8080 vs 8081), missing vasp-03. Update to match current 5-model zoo. See audit issue 6.1.

original_title: Fix deploy-dogfood.sh lockfile deletion anti-pattern. deploy-dogfood.sh line 59 deletes /tmp/dogfood-loop.lock, but dogfood-loop.sh comments warn 'IMPORTANT: Never delete the lockfile\! flock works on inodes, not paths.' Deleting creates race conditions with concurrent loops. Fix: only kill processes, let flock release naturally. See audit issue 6.2.
required_outcomes: Fix deploy-dogfood.sh lockfile deletion anti-pattern. deploy-dogfood.sh line 59 deletes /tmp/dogfood-loop.lock, but dogfood-loop.sh comments warn 'IMPORTANT: Never delete the lockfile\! flock works on inodes, not paths.' Deleting creates race conditions with concurrent loops. Fix: only kill processes, let flock release naturally. See audit issue 6.2.

original_description:
Clean up legacy variant references in Rust code and scripts. Several legacy model/variant names persist in the codebase despite being operationally dead:

1. config.rs default SWARM_FAST_MODEL is still 'OmniCoder-9B' (line ~15) — should be 'GLM-4.7-Flash' to match reality
2. scripts/run-gepa.py references omnicoder_9b and omnicoder_fixer variants
3. Possible 

**Note:** Previous attempt exhausted all iterations without making changes. Focus on making targeted edits early rather than extensive reading.

original_title: Audit and clean stale TZ feedback data (omnicoder_9b, strand_rust_14b)
original_description: The TZ Autopilot analysis shows strong performance for omnicoder_9b (0.753 task_resolved, n=2742) and strand_rust_14b (1.0, n=104), but codebase investigation reveals: (1) OmniCoder-9B is legacy/dead — replaced by GLM-4.7-Flash, no longer deployed on any node. (2) strand_rust_14b does NOT exist anywhere in the codebase — likely a prototype variant that was never merged. This stale feedback data could corrupt Thompson Sampling if these variants are ever accidentally re-added. Action items: (1) Query TZ Postgres to confirm the date range of omnicoder/strand_rust feedback — is it all historical? (2) If TZ supports variant archival or data exclusion, mark these variants as archived so their feedback doesn't influence future experimentation. (3) Document in tensorzero.toml [retired] section that omnicoder_9b and strand_rust_14b have stale high-performance data that should not be trusted. (4) If strand_rust_14b was a real model (possibly Strand-Rust from Jarvislabs?), note what it was for future reference.
required_outcomes: Audit and clean stale TZ feedback data (omnicoder_9b, strand_rust_14b)

original_description: Location: coordination/src/verifier/risk_profile.rs:304

original_title: Fix performance issues: redundant I/O, unnecessary clones, DRY violations
original_description:
Several performance and DRY violations identified.

## Critical: Redundant disk I/O
- mutation_archive.rs: Every query method (query_by_error, query_similar, query_anti_patterns, model_success_rate, ucb_score, etc.) calls load_all() which reads+parses the entire JSONL file from disk. A single orchestration cycle hits load_all() 3+ times via context_for_issue(). Add in-memory cache with invalidation.

## DRY violations:
- verifier/safety_scan.rs: scan_diff() and scan_diff_text() duplicate ~50 lines of scanning logic. Refactor scan_diff() to call get_diff_text() then delegate to scan_diff_text().
- work_packet/generator.rs:199-265: Four nearly identical loops for extracting structs/traits/enums/functions. Create SYMBOL_PATTERNS table.

## Unnecessary clones:
- verifier/report.rs:270-271: g.errors.clone() in flat_map. Use references instead.
- modes/contextual.rs:140-142: history_vec() clones entire VecDeque<Message> into Vec on every step. Messages can be large.
- cognition.rs:119-121: item.clone() inside retrieve() clones before filtering.
- search_code_tool.rs:72-73: args.pattern.clone() and args.glob.clone() appear unnecessary.

## HTTP client per call:
- fim.rs:44-74: complete_fim creates new reqwest::Client on every call. Accept &Client parameter or use OnceLock.

## Redundant computation:
- agent_profile.rs:144-398: default_matrix() rebuilds 40-entry matrix from scratch on every call. Cache with OnceLock.

## Unnecessary allocations:
- fs_tools.rs:269-287: has_json_escape_sequences calls .to_ascii_lowercase() unnecessarily.
- embedding.rs:66-117: embed_batch uses manual temp file naming (race-prone). Use tempfile::NamedTempFile.
- events/types.rs:144-186: EnsembleEvent::timestamp() has exhaustive match boilerplate. Consider macro.
required_outcomes: Fix performance issues: redundant I/O, unnecessary clones, DRY violations

original_title: Extract magic values to named constants with rationale docs (M-DOCUMENTED-MAGIC)
original_description:
Numerous hardcoded values lack named constants and rationale documentation.

## coordination/ magic values:
- main.rs:86-99: Hardcoded legacy model names ('OR1-Behemoth.Q8_0', etc.)
- main.rs:77: Default URL 'http://10.0.0.31:8000/v1/chat/completions'
- resilience.rs:261,276: Magic number 3 for recovery/degradation thresholds
- perf_control.rs:110-119: Defaults 10_000, 200, 65_536, 10
- perf_control.rs:180-190: Retry defaults 500ms, 2.0x, 5000ms, 2 retries
- speculation.rs:34-58: max_proposals: 3, token_budget: 200_000
- speculation.rs:62-83: Env var name strings inline
- reformulation.rs:81: 180_000 token threshold
- verifier/pipeline.rs:76-77: gate_timeout_secs: 1800, stderr_max_bytes: 4096
- context_packer/packer.rs:33-37: SPAN_CONTEXT_LINES: 80, SMALL_FILE_THRESHOLD: 200
- feedback/correction_loop.rs:19-28: max_iterations: 5, escalation_threshold: 2
- ensemble/coordinator.rs:70-78: temperature 0.3, max_tokens 2048, timeout 300s
- ensemble/voting.rs:50: LOW_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD needs doc comment
- events/bus.rs:14: CHANNEL_CAPACITY: 256 needs rationale
- analytics weights and thresholds (replay.rs, skills.rs)

## crates/swarm-agents/ magic values:
- confidence.rs:19: DEFAULT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD: 0.55 needs rationale
- confidence.rs:87: Hardcoded 0.3 for verbal uncertainty
- context_firewall.rs:98: RESPONSE_TAIL_BUDGET: 500 needs rationale
- knowledge_sync.rs:82: TRICKY_BUG_THRESHOLD: 3 needs rationale
- action_validator.rs:451: braces > 2 threshold
- exec_tool.rs:45-48: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECS and TEST_TIMEOUT_SECS both 1800

## Approach:
- Extract inline values to const items with /// doc comments
- Document WHY the value was chosen and side effects of changing it
- Group related constants near the types that use them
required_outcomes: Extract magic values to named constants with rationale docs (M-DOCUMENTED-MAGIC)
main.rs:86-99: Hardcoded legacy model names ('OR1-Behemoth.Q8_0', etc.)
resilience.rs:261,276: Magic number 3 for recovery/degradation thresholds
speculation.rs:62-83: Env var name strings inline
Extract inline values to const items with /// doc comments
Document WHY the value was chosen and side effects of changing it
Group related constants near the types that use them

original_title: Add pre-flight model health probes before worker delegation
original_description: Before each worker delegation, probe the target model endpoint with a tiny completion request (not just /health). If the model doesn't respond within 2s, mark it unhealthy and route to next variant. This prevents the omnicoder hung-model cascading failure where TS routed 100% to a hung model. Integrate with cluster_health.rs. Design doc: docs/research/self-improving-swarm-architecture.md Layer 2.
required_outcomes: Add pre-flight model health probes before worker delegation

original_title: Implement health-gated TZ feedback to prevent data poisoning
original_description: Before posting feedback to TZ via post_episode_feedback(), check whether the failure was infrastructure (tokio panic, endpoint down, context overflow, empty response, timeout) or model quality (verifier failed, max iterations, circuit breaker). Only post feedback for model quality failures and successes. Infrastructure failures should be logged but not counted as model performance signal. This prevents the Apr 7-10 data poisoning that required manual deletion of 36K false negatives. Design doc: docs/research/self-improving-swarm-architecture.md Layer 1.
required_outcomes: Implement health-gated TZ feedback to prevent data poisoning

original_title: Migrate worktree lifecycle to bd worktree create/remove
original_description:
Our worktree_bridge.rs manually manages git worktrees with custom beads redirect.
bd worktree create handles the full lifecycle: create, configure beads redirect, track state.

Implementation:
1. Replace git worktree add/remove calls with bd worktree create/remove
2. Remove custom .beads redirect logic from worktree_bridge.rs
3. Gain: automatic beads database sharing, proper cleanup on failure
4. bd worktree list replaces our manual /tmp/beefcake-wt/ scanning

Key files: crates/swarm-agents/src/worktree_bridge.rs
required_outcomes: Migrate worktree lifecycle to bd worktree create/remove

original_title: Add efficiency-aware routing: compare top variants on cost/latency, not just success
original_description:
Current Thompson Sampling optimizes solely on task_resolved (boolean success). This can select expensive/slow winners over slightly-less-successful but much faster alternatives.

Example from autopilot data: if sera_14b_worker (77.4% resolved, fast) is marginally below a hypothetical variant on resolution but 3x faster, it's the better operational default. We already track wall_time_seconds and iterations_used as TZ metrics but don't use them in routing.

Action:
1. Query TZ Postgres for per-variant efficiency comparison:
   SELECT variant_name, 
     AVG(CASE WHEN metric_name='task_resolved' AND value=1 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) as success_rate,
     AVG(CASE WHEN metric_name='wall_time_seconds' THEN value END) as avg_wall_time,
     AVG(CASE WHEN metric_name='iterations_used' THEN value END) as avg_iterations
   FROM feedback f JOIN inferences i ON f.inference_id = i.id
   WHERE function_name = 'worker_code_edit'
   GROUP BY variant_name

2. Document the success-vs-efficiency tradeoff for top 3 worker variants
3. If a variant is within 5% success but 2x+ faster, consider:
   a. Switching experimentation metric to iteration_efficiency (composite)
   b. Or using a weighted multi-objective: 0.7*task_resolved + 0.3*(1/normalized_wall_time)
4. Update TZ config experimentation section if a better metric is identified
5. Same analysis for code_fixing variants

Note: TZ Thompson Sampling only supports single-metric optimization. Multi-objective requires either a composite metric or manual weight adjustment in the static_weights fallback.
required_outcomes: Add efficiency-aware routing: compare top variants on cost/latency, not just success

original_title: Add Debug impls to ~60+ public types across both crates (M-PUBLIC-DEBUG)
original_description:
The most pervasive violation. ~60+ public types lack Debug implementations.

## coordination/ types needing Debug:
- benchmark_tools.rs: BenchmarkState, BenchmarkTools
- perf_control.rs: PerfGuard
- patch.rs: PatchEngine, ApplyResult
- ensemble/coordinator.rs: EnsembleCoordinator
- ensemble/arbitration.rs: ArbitrationManager
- ensemble/context.rs: ContextManager
- ensemble/voting.rs: VotingProtocol
- harness/feature_registry.rs: FeatureRegistry
- harness/git_manager.rs: GitManager
- harness/progress.rs: ProgressTracker
- harness/tools.rs: HarnessState
- debate/memory_bridge.rs: DebateMemoryBridge
- debate/orchestrator.rs: DebateOrchestrator
- debate/persistence.rs: CheckpointManager
- memory/budget.rs: CompactionTrigger
- memory/compactor.rs: MemoryCompactor
- memory/summarizer.rs: MockSummarizer
- analytics/replay.rs: TraceIndex
- analytics/skills.rs: SkillLibrary
- analytics/verification.rs: VerificationTracker
- events/bus.rs: EventBus
- events/history.rs: EventHistory
- registry/mod.rs: ProviderRegistry
- router/prompts.rs: FixPromptBuilder, GenerationPromptBuilder, SystemPrompts

## crates/swarm-agents/ types needing Debug:
- endpoint_pool.rs: EndpointPool
- batch.rs: BatchExecute
- workpad_tool.rs: AnnounceTool, CheckAnnouncementsTool
- All 18+ tool structs (ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, etc.)
- All 20+ tool argument structs (ApplyPlanArgs, AstGrepInput, etc.)
- beads_bridge.rs: BeadsBridge
- worktree_bridge.rs: WorktreeBridge
- notebook_bridge.rs: NotebookBridge
- cognition.rs: CognitionBase
- meta_reflection.rs: MetaReflector
- mutation_archive.rs: MutationArchive
- experiment_db.rs: ExperimentDb
- autopilot.rs: AutopilotRunner
- context_firewall.rs: CondensedAgentTool

## Approach:
- Use #[derive(Debug)] where all fields implement Debug
- Use manual Debug impl where fields don't (e.g., Arc<dyn Trait>, reqwest::Client)
- For types holding secrets (CloudEndpoint, Endpoint), implement Debug with REDACTION of api_key fields
- Mark compliant files with // Rust guideline compliant 2026-02-21
required_outcomes: Add Debug impls to ~60+ public types across both crates (M-PUBLIC-DEBUG)
All 20+ tool argument structs (ApplyPlanArgs, AstGrepInput, etc.)
Use #[derive(Debug)] where all fields implement Debug
Use manual Debug impl where fields don't (e.g., Arc<dyn Trait>, reqwest::Client)
For types holding secrets (CloudEndpoint, Endpoint), implement Debug with REDACTION of api_key fields
Mark compliant files with // Rust guideline compliant 2026-02-21

iteration_efficiency is defined in config/tensorzero.toml as an episode-level metric with optimize=min, but no code ever posts it. This is dead config that wastes TZ's metric processing.

Action:
1. In orchestrator/mod.rs near line 4021 where other episode metrics are posted, add iteration_efficiency = iterations_used / max_retries (a normalized 0-1 score)
2. Post via post_episode_feedback alongside the existing metrics
3. Alternatively, if the metric isn't useful for routing decisions, remove it from tensorzero.toml to reduce noise
